caption_spanish: Billio ahora conecta modelos Ollama locales a un servidor MCP en la nube — tus datos financieros nunca salen de tu máquina.
hook: Your financial data stays on your machine. The AI that reads it does too.
visual_instruction: Branded local-first AI architecture diagram showing the Local Ollama (Gemma 4) model connected to a cloud MCP server and Firestore database via a Node.js terminal bridge.
mermaid_code:
graph LR
    A["Local Ollama\n(Gemma 4)"] -->|"Inference"| B["Terminal Bridge\n(Node.js)"]
    B -->|"Bearer Token Auth"| C["MCP Server\n(Cloud Functions)"]
    C -->|"Tool Calls"| D["Firestore\n(Ledger Data)"]
    D -->|"Results"| C
    C -->|"Response"| B
    B -->|"Display"| A
    
    style A fill:#1e1b4b,stroke:#6366f1,color:#e0e7ff
    style B fill:#0c121d,stroke:#06b6d4,color:#e0e7ff
    style C fill:#1e1b4b,stroke:#6366f1,color:#e0e7ff
    style D fill:#0c121d,stroke:#06b6d4,color:#e0e7ff
caption_english:
Your financial data stays on your machine. The AI that reads it does too. And it can be 100% free!

Billio now connects a locally-hosted model (i.e. Gemma 4) to a cloud MCP server through a terminal-based bridge script using ollama or opencode (also free). The flow:

1. You pick your model from installed Ollama instances (or set OLLAMA_MODEL env var)
2. The script authenticates against the deployed MCP endpoint with a persisted Bearer token
3. Ollama runs inference locally with an 8K context window — large enough for full ledger analysis
4. Tool calls are routed to Firebase through the MCP protocol, results flow back into the local conversation

No data leaves your machine except structured MCP tool calls. Model selection is persistent — stored in a local .mcp_model file so you pick once, then just chat.

The key architecture decision: separating the inference layer (local) from the data layer (cloud) through MCP's standardized tool-calling interface. This means you can swap models without touching a single line of server code.

More at bervos.org

#BuildInPublic #LocalLLM #MCPProtocol #Firebase #IndieHacker

Stop switching between 10 different monitoring tabs just to track your projects health.

Managing scattered #Firebase consoles and various repositories created a constant bottleneck in my operational workflow. I built BERVOS Hub to unify these data streams into a single dashboard for real-time visibility.

• Integrated GitHub API for centralized commit tracking and deployment status.
• Utilized Firebase Admin SDK to aggregate telemetry data into a unified schema.
• Implemented SWR for seamless data revalidation and responsive UI performance.

Explore the full technical architecture at bervos.org/hub. See screenshot!

hook: Old photos have no GPS data. But they have visual clues.
caption_english:
Old photos have no GPS data. But they have visual clues.

Pinmage analyzes scanned photo albums using AI vision to deduce where a photo was taken. The location pipeline has 4 fallback layers:

1. AI Vision (#Gemini or local #Ollama) analyzes the image → extracts a place name and coordinates
2. OSM Nominatim (primary geocoder) → converts the place name to coordinates
3. Apple CLGeocoder (secondary) → fallback when Nominatim misses
4. AI-provided coordinates (last resort) → validated with isValidCoordinate()

Each layer has its own cache to avoid redundant lookups. The Nominatim rate limiter uses a minimum 1-second interval between requests — OSM's terms of service, respected.

Why the cascade? No single geocoder handles everything. Nominatim excels at specific places. CLGeocoder handles regional queries better. AI coordinates are surprisingly accurate for landmark-adjacent photos.

Result: ~85% of scanned photos from the 1990s now have GPS metadata written directly to EXIF.

More at pinmage.bervos.org

#SwiftUI #Gemini #Ollama #macOS #OpenStreetMap
caption_spanish: Pinmage uses a 4-layer geocoding cascade to reverse-engineer GPS coordinates from old, scanned photo albums.
visual_instruction: Generate a branded image showing the geocoding cascade as a vertical waterfall/funnel. Top: AI Vision eye icon. Layer 2: OSM Nominatim globe. Layer 3: Apple maps pin. Layer 4: AI coordinates as last resort. Dark bg, indigo/cyan accent connections. Mono label '// UNDER_THE_HOOD // PINMAGE'. Also prepare Ray.so screenshot of PinmageManager.swift showing the AI lat/lon fallback block (~8 lines starting at 'AI lat/lon as last-resort fallback').
mermaid_code:
graph TD
    A["Photo Input\n(No GPS)"] --> B["AI Vision\n(Gemini / Ollama)"]
    B -->|"Place name"| C["OSM Nominatim\n(1 req/sec)"]
    C -->|"Found?"| D{"Coordinates?"}
    D -->|"Yes"| H["Write EXIF\nGPS Metadata"]
    D -->|"No"| E["Apple CLGeocoder"]
    E -->|"Found?"| F{"Coordinates?"}
    F -->|"Yes"| H
    F -->|"No"| G["AI-provided\nLat/Lon"]
    G -->|"isValidCoordinate()"| H
    
    style A fill:#0c121d,stroke:#06b6d4,color:#e0e7ff
    style B fill:#1e1b4b,stroke:#6366f1,color:#e0e7ff
    style C fill:#0c121d,stroke:#06b6d4,color:#e0e7ff
    style H fill:#1e1b4b,stroke:#6366f1,color:#e0e7ff

hook: Plotting 10 years of photos on a map is easy. Making it fly between continents smoothly is not.
caption_english:
Plotting 10 years of photos on a map is easy. Making it fly between continents smoothly is not.

Aura's photo heatmap has a 'Time Tour' playback mode — it animates through your photos chronologically, flying the map to each location. The first version flickered badly.

The problem: every animation frame was removing ALL map annotations and re-adding them. Even if only one cluster changed. Apple's #MapKit diffing was non-existent — we had to build our own in #SwiftUI.

The fix: optimized annotation diffing.

• Calculate toRemove: annotations in old set but not in new set
• Calculate toAdd: annotations in new set but not in old set  
• Calculate toUpdate: same ID but different count → remove old, add new
• Only touch what actually changed

Then the distance problem: jumping from Tokyo to Buenos Aires in one animated step looked jarring. Solution: two-step flyover zoom. If distance > 15°, first zoom out to a midpoint region, then zoom into the target. 550ms between steps.

Result: smooth, cinematic playback across a decade of travel photos.

More at aura.bervos.org

#SwiftUI #MapKit #macOS #AppleDev #WebDev
caption_spanish: Aura's Time Tour mode performs smooth, two-step map transitions between photo locations across different continents.
visual_instruction: Generate a branded image showing a world map with photo clusters connected by curved flight paths. Dark background, indigo dots for clusters, cyan arced lines for flight paths. Include a small playback control bar at bottom. Mono label '// VIBE_CODING_REALITY // AURA'. Also prepare Ray.so screenshot of PhotosHeatmapView.swift showing the toRemove/toAdd/toUpdate diffing logic (~12 lines).

caption_spanish: tripitdown has been updated to support multi-thread conversation history, automated session titling, and a transparent error-handling fallback system for improved travel planning reliability.
hook: One chat thread per trip wasn't enough. Neither was losing context between sessions.

caption_english:
Travel planning shouldn't collapse under the weight of a single chat thread. 

Managing flight logistics, dining reservations, and site-seeing within one context window leads to information loss and fragmented planning. tripitdown now implements structured session management to maintain state across complex travel itineraries using #Firebase for backend persistence. 

• Multi-thread support allows segmentation of trip logistics into dedicated conversations. 
• Automated session titling using Gemini 3.5 Flash simplifies navigation. 
• Transparent fallback logic ensures service continuity if a model returns a 503 error. 
• Integrated token tracking provides visibility into #GeminiAPI usage costs per project. 

View the technical documentation and project status at tripitdown.bervos.org. 

#GeminiAPI #Firebase #CloudFunctions #SoftwareArchitecture #APIIntegration

hook: Supporting Arabic, Japanese, and Elvish wasn't enough. Morse code forced a completely different rendering approach.
caption_english:
Supporting Arabic, Japanese, and Elvish wasn't enough. Morse code forced a completely different rendering approach.

Scribo helps you practice writing systems through spaced repetition. Each system renders differently:
• Arabic/Japanese: Unicode text glyphs
• Elvish (Tengwar): Custom web font
• Morse: Can't use text at all

Morse code is visual — dots and dashes. So I built MorseDisplay: a #React component that renders each symbol as a sized div. Dots are 5×5px circles, dashes are 14×5px rounded rectangles. Three size variants (large/small/mini) for different contexts.

The harder problem: mixed practice sessions. When you shuffle Arabic + Morse + Elvish, the renderer needs per-item catalog detection to pick the right component. The solution: each practice item carries a `catalog` field, and the render switch checks it before choosing between text, font, or visual display.

Also shipped: dynamic reporting windows (7d/30d/90d/all/custom), session history persistence to #Firestore, and Greek alphabet support.

526 lines added for Morse alone.

More at scribo.bervos.org

#React #Firestore #WebDev #TypeScript
caption_spanish: Scribo adds Morse code spaced-repetition training using custom visual SVG dot/dash components.
visual_instruction: Generate a branded image showing different writing systems side by side on dark cards: Arabic character, Japanese kana, Elvish tengwar letter, and Morse code dots/dashes. Dark bg, each card with a subtle border. The Morse card should show visual dots and dashes (not text). Mono label '// VIBE_CODING_REALITY // SCRIBO'. Also prepare Ray.so screenshot of the MorseDisplay component from Practice.jsx (~15 lines showing the dot/dash rendering logic).

hook: A Chrome extension that downloads YouTube audio, tags it with iTunes metadata, and now embeds lyrics — all locally.
caption_english:
A #Chrome extension that downloads YouTube audio, tags it with #iTunes metadata, and now embeds lyrics — all locally.

YT2MP3's download pipeline now has 5 stages:

1. Chrome Extension captures the YouTube URL
2. Local #NodeJS server receives it via localhost
3. yt-dlp extracts audio, ffmpeg converts to MP3
4. iTunes Search API finds the correct metadata (artist, album, cover art)
5. LRCLIB API fetches lyrics — tries artist+title first, falls back to general query

The lyrics integration was cleaner than expected. LRCLIB returns either plain or synced (timestamped) lyrics. If only synced is available, we strip the timestamps with a regex: `.replace(/\[\d+:\d+(?:\.\d+)?\]/g, '')`

Also added: silent server wake-up on popup load (so the extension feels instant), auto-open files after download, and a search hint input for when YouTube titles are too messy for iTunes matching.

Everything runs on your machine. No cloud processing, no subscriptions.

More at github.com/laresbernardo/YT2MP3

#ChromeExtension #NodeJS #iTunes #LRCLIB #JavaScript
caption_spanish: YT2MP3 automatically tags downloaded YouTube audio with iTunes metadata and embeds lyrics from LRCLIB.

hook: If an LLM can't find your project, it can't recommend it.
caption_english:
If an LLM can't find your project, it can't recommend it.

BERVOS suffered from a lack of machine-readable data, leaving the project invisible to search engines and AI models. Without structured metadata, discovery via #SEO and LLM indexing remained impossible.

• Automated JSON-LD generation for Schema.org SoftwareApplication markup.
• Implemented llms.txt and llms-full.txt standards for direct AI discovery.
• Integrated automated sitemap.xml and robots.txt maintenance.
• Added a custom DecryptedText character animation component for improved UI engagement.

One command now synchronizes all metadata across the entire ecosystem. Check out the implementation details at bervos.org.

hook: Scraped DOM elements are too fragile for WhatsApp chat exports. We built a background service that safely intercepts and formats raw payloads.
caption_english:
WhatsApp Web renders lazily, meaning direct DOM extraction drops half the conversation context. We needed a decoupled extension architecture.

By offloading serialization to a Chrome extension background service, we prevent UI thread blocks and secure full conversation logs without memory leaks.

Get the source details at bervos.org.

Resumen en español: Diseñamos un servicio en segundo plano para WAme que intercepta y exporta chats de WhatsApp Web sin bloquear el hilo principal de la interfaz.

#ChromeExtension #TypeScript #WebScraping #BuildInPublic #SoftwareArchitecture
caption_spanish: Diseñamos un servicio en segundo plano para WAme que intercepta y exporta chats de WhatsApp Web sin bloquear el hilo principal de la interfaz.

hook: When your primary LLM throws a 503 error mid-travel-planning, you don't crash the client. You failover.
caption_english:
Rate limits and model outages shouldn't stall a user's trip generation. Relying on a single API endpoint is a single point of failure.

We implemented an automated fallback routing decorator. If the primary model times out or returns a 503, the app silently retries using a secondary lightweight endpoint.

Latency increases by 1.2s on failover, but reliability hits 99.9%.

Test the flow at tripitdown.bervos.org.

Resumen en español: tripitdown implementa un sistema de reintentos automáticos que cambia a un modelo de respaldo cuando la IA principal falla.

#API #ResilienceEngineering #TypeScript #GeminiAPI #IndieHacker
caption_spanish: tripitdown implementa un sistema de reintentos automáticos que cambia a un modelo de respaldo cuando la IA principal falla.

Minimalist code screenshot (Ray.so dark theme) showing the automated JS error handling catch-block and API fallback route assignment with a cyan glow highlight around the fallback trigger code.

hook: LocalStorage is great until your user clears their browser cache and loses years of music theory progress.
caption_english:
Our offline-first approach relied too heavily on local storage, creating zero-data sync issues across devices.

We migrated to Firestore persistence with structured schemas, supporting offline cache-first reads and a one-time migration script.

Zero data loss across device switches.

Read more at bervos.org.

Resumen en español: Migramos el almacenamiento de Tonaly a Firestore para evitar la pérdida de datos del usuario y sincronizar el progreso entre dispositivos.

#Firebase #Firestore #MusicTheory #TypeScript #IndieDev

hook: If photo N has GPS data, and photo N+2 does too, why does photo N+1 have blank metadata?
caption_english:
Bulk importing non-GPS old family photos leaves annoying gaps in map timelines.

We built a timeline extrapolation algorithm. Pinmage now detects close-timestamp groupings and inherits coordinates with structural validation.

Try it at pinmage.bervos.org.

#SwiftUI #iOSDev #Geocoding #Metadata #CoreLocation

hook: Splitting "Beyoncé & Shakira" is easy. Splitting "Simon & Garfunkel" ruins your music analytics.
caption_english:
Parsing audio metadata for top artist charts has a major pain point: compound artist tags. If you split naively by commas, ampersands, or "feat.", you end up with "Garfunkel" as a solo artist, or "Earth" and "Wind" as separate entities from "Earth, Wind & Fire".

To solve this in Aura, we implemented ArtistResolver. Instead of regular expression hacks, we built a rule-based parser combined with a localized dictionary of known compound bands. It runs on the client, preserving formal naming conventions while cleanly attributing collaborations to individual profiles for accurate listening metrics.

This keeps our core UI charts fast, clean, and lexicographically correct without sending metadata to a heavy cloud parser.

Learn more about Aura's music statistics engine at bervos.org.

#SwiftUI #iOSDev #MusicAnalytics #DataEngineering #BuildInPublic
caption_spanish: Un analizador basado en reglas en Swift resuelve la división de artistas colaboradores sin destruir los nombres de bandas icónicas en la biblioteca de Aura.

Layout: A clean architecture flowchart displaying the ArtistResolver logic parsing "Simon & Garfunkel" (retains as one group) vs "Beyoncé & Shakira" (splits into two tokens).

    B -- No --> D[Scan for Delimiters: &, feat., x]

    E --> F[Trim & Normalize Naming]

    F --> G

hook: Chrome extension sandboxes can't trigger local file openings. So we went native.
caption_english:
A browser extension is structurally isolated from your operating system. When downloading media files, forcing users to open their Finder or File Explorer to play them ruins the user flow.

We bypassed this sandbox limitation by connecting the Chrome extension popup to a local native-messaging python server. Once the node download controller completes the writing of the audio file to the local directory, it fires a system-level command execution directly to open the default system audio player.

No manual searching. One click to download, convert to MP3, tag metadata, and open instantly in your local audio player.

Check out the YT2MP3 installation steps at bervos.org.

#ChromeExtension #Python #NodeJS #SystemProgramming #IndieHacker
caption_spanish: Superamos las limitaciones de la sandbox de Chrome conectando la extensión a un servidor nativo local en Python para abrir archivos MP3 descargados al instante.

Typography: bold white sans-serif headlines, mono-spaced labels in indigo-300. No stock photos, no people, no hands. Professional and premium feel. Square format (1080x1080px). Layout: A side-by-side comparison diagram. Before: Extension -> Sandboxed Browser Download -> Manual Directory Hunt. After: Extension -> Native Bridge Message -> Local Server -> Auto System Open.

hook: Navigating away mid-session shouldn't discard your language practice data.
caption_english:
In web apps, capturing active progress before a user leaves a page is notoriously unreliable. Standard React useEffect cleanups firing on component unmount often trigger async requests that get cancelled when the page context is torn down.

For Scribo's multi-lingual practice modules, losing a 20-character session's worth of progress due to an accidental dashboard navigation was a persistent friction point.

We refactored this by utilizing Firestore transaction writes and robust local storage caching. On component unmount, we cache the state synchronously to an offline queue first, then trigger a fire-and-forget sync utility. Even if the network thread is interrupted during route transition, the pending session synchronizes safely in the background the moment the app mounts the next view.

Start learning characters reliably on scribo.bervos.org.

#ReactJS #Firestore #WebDevelopment #TypeScript #BuildInPublic
caption_spanish: Implementamos un sistema de caché síncrono local que persiste el progreso de aprendizaje en Scribo antes de un montado de componente inestable.

Accent colors: electric indigo (#6366f1) and cyan (#06b6d4) used sparingly for highlights, borders, and glow effects. Style: minimal, clean, HUD-inspired, no clutter. Typography: bold white sans-serif headlines, mono-spaced labels in indigo-300. No stock photos, no people, no hands. Professional and premium feel. Square format (1080x1080px). Layout: High-contrast split screen showing a Ray.so style code editor with the clean unmount listener function caching to localStorage before syncing to Firestore.

hook: If you don't track your LLM token consumption per project, your API bill is a black box.
caption_english:
Running multi-model LLM features across several user travel planners gets expensive fast. Without granular usage logs, you have zero visibility into which prompt templates or models are burning through your credit limits.

In tripitdown, we engineered a native project-level and model-level token usage tracking dashboard. Every single completions call intercepts the raw API response meta tags (usage.prompt_tokens and usage.completion_tokens), aggregates the payload, and maps the math directly to real-time dollar amounts using dynamic model pricing structures in the frontend.

Users can now view an exact breakdown of what a specific itinerary cost to generate, grouped by project, allowing us to profile prompt efficiency and catch spiraling chat threads immediately.

Optimize your travels and monitor LLM budgets on tripitdown.bervos.org.

#LLMOps #GeminiAPI #React #IndieHacker #FintechEngineering
caption_spanish: Agregamos un panel de control local en tripitdown que calcula y desglosa el costo real de tokens consumidos por modelo y proyecto en tiempo real.

hook: OCR engines extract raw text, but they cannot reconcile financial accounts.
caption_english:
Parsing receipts often returns unstructured data like 'Visa *4321' instead of matching an active ledger account uuid. We updated the Billio intelligence schema to map payment metadata directly to database account profiles during the ingestion phase.

By implementing strict account awareness within our backend receipt schemas, our parser extracts the payment method and validates it against current credit account balances. This structural change prevents orphan transactions and maintains the integrity of the double-entry accounting model.

Now, incoming expense documents are automatically assigned to the correct source asset or liability account, eliminating manual categorization steps.

Review the schema definitions at billio.bervos.org

#BuildInPublic #TypeScript #Firebase #DatabaseArchitecture #JSONSchema
caption_spanish: Actualizamos el esquema de inteligencia de Billio para clasificar automáticamente transacciones de recibos y asociarlas directamente con cuentas contables reales.

  E --> F[(Firestore Database)]

hook: Static map pins degrade UI rendering loops when handling hundreds of custom coordinates.
caption_english:
In Pinmage, custom metadata editing means managing arbitrary coordinates that standard maps struggle to render smoothly. We resolved this performance bottleneck by building FavouritePlacesMapView with localized geofencing and custom location renaming.

Instead of triggering full MapKit context invalidations on every pin update, we refactored the map state to use granular model diffing. This isolates UI state updates to changed coordinates alone, maintaining a fluid 60fps frame rate during zooms and panning operations.

Users can now customize locations, assign specific labels, and preserve spatial parameters with zero render latency.

Download the desktop application at pinmage.bervos.org

#SwiftUI #MapKit #iOSDev #IndieHacker #StateManagement
caption_spanish: Creamos FavouritePlacesMapView en Pinmage para permitir la gestión y cambio de nombre de coordenadas sin comprometer la fluidez de renderizado en SwiftUI.

  B -->|Modified Coordinates| D[Targeted Marker Re-render]
  C & D --> E[Fluid Map View Render]

hook: A travel planning map showing fifteen overlapping routes is visually unreadable.
caption_english:
When planning multiple-stop itineraries in tripitdown, displaying every transit route at the same time turns the UI into a cluttered mess of lines. We decoupled route generation from the active rendering tree.

We implemented a localized visibility engine with manual path toggles. This allows users to selectively display transit coordinates while running an origin-aware rendering logic behind the scenes.

This update cuts down DOM complexity in our map components, ensuring optimal UI performance on mobile screens without sacrificing critical route context.

Build your itineraries at tripitdown.bervos.org

#React #TypeScript #WebMaps #FrontEndDev #Performance
caption_spanish: Añadimos controles de visibilidad interactivos para rutas complejas de viaje en tripitdown, reduciendo la carga de renderizado móvil.

hook: Chrome sandboxes block extensions from interacting directly with your local operating system.
caption_english:
A browser add-on cannot launch a native desktop application. To bridge this divide, YT2MP3 uses a lightweight local Node/Python host process configured to listen for secure extension requests.

We implemented an option that triggers our native server controller to auto-open files upon download completion. When the download manager finishes, a payload is fired over WebSocket to launch the default system media player with the new audio path.

This architecture bypasses typical browser isolation limitations, reducing down-to-play latency to zero while keeping the execution sandbox secure.

Check out the desktop client setup at yt2mp3.bervos.org

#JavaScript #Python #ChromeExtension #WebSockets #SystemsProgramming
caption_spanish: Diseñamos un puente nativo en YT2MP3 que permite abrir automáticamente archivos de audio descargados saltándose las restricciones del sandbox del navegador.
